entry.title: Integration Modernization @ SAP TechEd - A Recap
entry.content:
The Integration Modernization Team was part of this year’s TechEd event in Berlin. 

Let’s check some highlights, especially the workshops in the context of integration modernization with SAP BTP Integration Suite.

IN161 | Modernize and transform your integration to the cloud

This Workshop gave us the opportunity to understand how companies can migrate from SAP Process Orchestration to the SAP Integration Suite.”

  • Assess and Plan: Evaluate existing scenarios and estimate migration effort with built-in assessment tools.
  • Automate Migration: Use semi-automated tooling and pattern-based templates to accelerate transformation.
  • Ensure Reliability: Leverage cloud pipelines for guaranteed delivery, error handling, and restart capabilities.

Content: https://github.com/SAP-samples/teched2025-IN161

IN163 | Implement Exactly Once In-Order Delivery in SAP Integration

This Workshop was focused on ensuring data consistency in message exchange by implementing Exactly Once In-Order (EOIO) delivery within SAP Integration Suite.

  • EOIO Concept: Guarantees messages are delivered without duplication and in their original sequence.
  • Implementation Options:
    • Exclusive Queues in Cloud Integration.
    • Partitioned Queues using Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) for high-scale scenarios.

Content: https://github.com/SAP-samples/teched2025-IN163

IN165 | Experience Event-Driven Integration with Advanced Event Mesh

In this Workshop we explored how SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh enables scalable, secure event-driven architectures with features like event replay, filtering, and multi-protocol support.

  • Core Concepts: Learn queues, topics, and filtering to build robust event-driven solutions.
  • Advanced Features: Explore event replay, distributed tracing, and fine-grained filtering for complex scenarios.
  • Enterprise Scale: AEM supports multi-cloud and on-premise deployments, offering high performance, security, and protocol support (JMS, REST, AMQP, MQTT).

Content: https://github.com/SAP-samples/teched2025-IN165

IN302 | Run Integration Scenarios in Private Landscapes with Edge Integration Cell

This Depp Dive explained how to extend SAP Integration Suite to private environments using Edge Integration Cell, enabling hybrid integration scenarios with full control and compliance.

  • Private Deployment: Run integration flows in your own data center or private cloud for sensitive workloads.
  • Hybrid Architecture: Combine cloud-based integration with on-premise execution for flexibility and security.
  • Enterprise Readiness: Ensure governance, monitoring, and lifecycle management across distributed landscapes.

Content: 

IN166 | Central Monitoring of Integration Scenarios with SAP Cloud ALM

This workshop gave us the opportunity to see how SAP Cloud ALM provides a central cockpit for monitoring integration scenarios across hybrid landscapes.

  • Integration & Exception Monitoring: Get an overview of failed messages and exceptions across SAP Integration Suite and connected systems.
  • Health Monitoring: Track critical resources like JMS queues and expiring certificates to prevent disruptions.
  • End-to-End Visibility: Drill down to single message details and jump context-sensitively to local monitoring for issue resolution.
  • Alerts & Notifications: Activate alerts and receive automated email notifications for proactive incident handling.

Content: https://github.com/SAP-samples/teched2025-IN161

 

There’s a lot to look forward, and we’ll see what 2026 brings with it:

SAP Innovation Guide: SAP Innovation Guide 

Integration Suite RoadMap: Integration Suite Road Map 

SAP Integration Modernization Community: SAP Integration Modernization Community 

We’d love to hear your thoughts and experiences with SAP Integration Suite. What challenges are you facing? How can we help you unlock the full potential of your IT ecosystem? Feel free to reach out to us via SAP Integration Services.

#Modernize #Integrate #Innovate – SAP Integration Modernization TEAM

entry.title: SAP TechEd : Comment ça se passe vraiment ?
entry.content:
Un format pensé pour apprendre et expérimenter

SAP TechEd est l’une des conférences phare de SAP. Elle est principalement dédiée à l’innovation technique dans l’écosystème SAP. L’événement dure 3 jours et mélange différents formats pour répondre à tous les profils techniques : développeurs, consultants, architectes et experts SAP.

Chaque journée suit un rythme assez similaire. Les participants choisissent librement leur programme parmi des centaines de sessions techniques. Le premier jour fait exception avec deux keynotes. La première marque le lancement officiel de l’évenement. La deuxième, en fin de journée, est dédiée aux annonces sur les nouvelles technologies et aux dernières innovations.

Le choix des sessions : à toi de construire ton agenda !

Contrairement aux autres évènements, c’est toi qui choisis. Les inscriptions aux sessions et workshops se font directement sur le site du TechEd ou via l’application SAP Events. C’est une étape importante, car certaines sessions ont un nombre de places limitées. Souvent les workshops se remplissent rapidement.

Les formats les plus pertinentes selon moi sont :

  • Sessions Deep Dive : 45 à 60 minutes pour explorer un sujet en profondeur (ex : ABAP Cloud, SAP BTP, IA, intégration, sécurité…)
  • Usability Testing : pour donner son avis sur les nouveaux outils et aider SAP à améliorer l’expérience utilisateur
  • Roadmap Sessions : pour comprendre où SAP va dans les prochains mois
  • Hands-On Workshops : pour tester directement les nouvelles technologies

Les workshops hands-on : le cœur de TechEd

Les hands-on c’est ce qui rend TechEd unique. Pas seulement de la théorie, mais aussi de la pratique avec les experts du produit.
Le déroulement classique de tous les workshops:

  1. Tu reçois un scénario ou un cas d’usage à suivre
  2. Tu accèdes à un environnement SAP de démonstration qui souvent déjà préconfiguré
  3. Tu développes ou configures pas à pas, avec l’aide d’experts
  4. Tu peux poser des questions en live si tu bloques aux responsables du produits.

⚠️Limite importante : SAP impose souvent un maximum de 2 workshops hands-on par personne. Une petite astuce existe pour en faire plus. Sur place, vous pouvez parfois récupérer une place libre le jour J en arrivant en avance. Ce n’est pas garanti, mais ça peut fonctionner.

Certifications

SAP met souvent à disposition des parcours dédiés, des sessions ciblées pour préparer des certifications. A la fin du TechEd des créneaux sont proposés pour passer examens sur place.

Ce qui peut être difficile

TechEd est un événement technique. Il peut être très dense pour des profils moins techniques. Autre difficulté plus opérationnels :

  • Les environnements peuvent être lents si beaucoup de monde se connecte en même temps
  • Il faut choisir, et renoncer à beaucoup de sessions intéressantes
  • L’agenda se remplit vite, surtout pour les workshops

Ce qui peut être intéressant

  • Bien se préparer en amont de l’évenement en lisant les annonces sur les sessions choisis
  • Si possible, faire quelques exercices sur la BTP

Le vrai plus : les rencontres

Le TechEd, c’est aussi :

  • Rencontrer les experts SAP que nous avons l’habitude de voir en ligne
  • Échanger avec des personnes qui vivent les mêmes projets que nous
  • Reconnecter la communauté technique dans la vraie vie
  • Partager des idées, des difficultés, des solutions

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2025 - Nouveautés & Retour personnel
entry.content:
Si Berlin est célèbre pour une chose, en dehors de ses clubs techno et de ses currywurst, c'est bien pour sa capacité à vous bousculer. Et c'est exactement ce qui s'est passé lors du SAP TechEd 2025. Dès que les lumières se sont allumées sur la scène, une chose était claire : ce n'était pas une conférence comme les autres. Berlin a apporté son énergie, SAP a apporté son innovation, et ensemble, ils ont offert une semaine qui a laissé tous les développeurs, consultants et architectes surexcités.

SAP TechEd a porté un message simple : le futur de SAP sera plus intelligent, tourné vers le cloud, et construit pour les développeurs. Pour la communauté SAP Paris, cet événement est plus qu'une conférence technologique mondiale, c'est une boussole. Il nous aide à anticiper les nouvelles compétences à développer. Il nous permet aussi de comprendre les projets et les innovations qui auront un impact sur nous, les consultants, développeurs, architectes, et décideurs.

SAP TechEd, fonctionnement

Ce blog regroupe tout ce qu’il faut savoir sur le fonctionnement de SAP TechEd.

Nouvelles actualités de SAP - The Flywheel

 

SAP réinvente sa méthode de travail en réunissant des assistants d'IA basés sur les rôles, un Business Data Cloud unifié et une SAP Business Suite améliorée, rendant les applications quotidiennes plus intelligentes, plus intuitives et alimentées par des informations en temps réel.

Principales annonces qui ont marqué SAP TechEd

Introduction à l'AI Foundation

SAP a annoncé le lancement de sa nouvelle plateforme d'IA, une couche unifiée de fonctionnalités d'IA prêtes à l'emploi dans tous ses environnements. Les principales innovations comprennent :

  • SAP-RPT-1 : un modèle relationnel pré entraîné qui fournit des informations prédictives précises à partir de données métier structurées.
  • Prompt optimizing : SAP a introduit un assistant intelligent pour optimiser les prompts.

  • AI for Europe : avec SAP, Mistral AI et Cohere North. Une nouvelle initiative qui garantit la conformité avec la loi européenne sur l'IA et les exigences régionales en matière de souveraineté des données.

Agentic AI

Il s'agit de systèmes IA capables de prendre des décisions et d'agir de manière autonome pour atteindre des objectifs complexes sans supervision humaine constante.

  • Agents Low-code dans joule studio / Agents Pro-code dans la BTP
  • Agent interoperability (A2A), Agent Hub & Agent Mining (Signavio)
  • Agentic Orchestration

ABAP Renaissance

Lors du TechEd, SAP a confirmé que l'écosystème ABAP entre dans une nouvelle ère : plus intelligent, natif du cloud et soutenu par l'IA.

 

  • Introduction de SAP-ABAP-1 : un modèle d’IA dédié formé sur les modèles ABAP
  • ABAP AI à partir de la version cloud privée SAP S/4HANA 2021+
  • ABAP Cloud development débarque sur VS Code
  • Développement conduit par agent pour ABAP activé via MCP

SAP Business Data Cloud

SAP a élargi son écosystème de données en renforçant son engagement en faveur de l'interopérabilité et de la gouvernance partagée entre les principales plateformes. Intégration avec Snowflake, Datasphere et Databricks

Retour personnel 2025

SAP TechEd était vraiment intéressant cette année, tant pour les annonces que pour les ateliers pratiques. C'était également le premier TechEd en présentiel depuis la COVID, et ça a fait une énorme différence. Rencontrer les personnes qui ont travaillé sur les outils SAP et discuter avec elles est vraiment enrichissant. J’ai pu leur poser des questions sur des problèmes concrets que j’avais rencontrés lors de mes explorations.

La plupart des annonces portaient sur l'IA, ce qui est logique compte tenu de l'orientation prise aujourd'hui par le monde de l'informatique. SAP s'est clairement engagé dans la voie de l'IA et tente de l'intégrer dans presque tous les domaines. L'IA a tellement été mise en avant que des sujets tels que Clean Core n'ont été que très brièvement abordés. Selon moi, elles étaient parfois loin de la réalité du terrain. Il faudra voir comment tout cela évolue avec le temps. Une chose est sûre : SAP a pris le virage de l’IA. Et je pense que nous devons tous le prendre aussi. Mais il ne faut pas oublier que l’avenir ne sera pas construit uniquement autour de l’IA. C'est un outil puissant. Il va changer nos façons de travailler mais ne remplacera pas tout.

Pour moi, cet événement est un incontournable pour tout consultant technique. J'ai beaucoup appris grâce aux ateliers et aux sessions approfondies. Si vous avez un jour l'occasion de participer à SAP TechEd, je vous le recommande vivement, car cette expérience en vaut vraiment la peine !

entry.title: 🚀 SAP Mini Inside Track – Chennai | Coming April 2026
entry.content:
We are excited to announce that the SAP Chennai Community is gearing up to host an upcoming SAP Mini Inside Track (SIT) in April 2026! 🎉

This community‑driven event will bring together SAP professionals, consultants, developers, architects, students, and enthusiasts from across Chennai and nearby regions to collaborate, learn, and share knowledge.

📍 Date & Venue:
Details will be announced very soon — stay tuned!

🌟 What to Expect at SAP Mini Inside Track – Chennai

✅ Cutting‑Edge SAP Topics
Explore insightful sessions covering the latest in SAP technologies, innovations, and real‑world experiences — including S/4HANA, BTP, integrations, cloud, AI, and more.

✅ Knowledge Sharing by the Community
Learn from local SAP experts and practitioners across various organizations who will share practical use cases, lessons learned, and best practices.

✅ Networking & Collaboration
Connect with fellow SAP professionals, exchange ideas, expand your network, and be part of meaningful conversations within the SAP ecosystem.

✅ Open Community Platform
Bring your thoughts, questions, and experiences — this is a collaborative space built by the community, for the community.

🔔 Stay Connected

More information on event agenda, speakers, registration, date, and venue will be shared shortly through this page.

Follow the SAP Chennai Community Page and keep an eye out for updates — exciting times ahead! 🙌

Let’s come together to learn, connect, and grow the SAP ecosystem in Chennai.

#SAPMiniInsideTrack #SAPChennai #SAPCommunity #SAPEvents
#InsideTrack #SAPLearning #SAPNetworking #ChennaiTech #SAPProfessionals

 

Just say the word 👍

Regards

Prasath

entry.title: Bookmark & Share - SAP free trials, Powerful demos and downloads
entry.content:
Get your hands on our free trials, demos and downloads and start building something great. Explore our most popular trials and downloads, or search for a specific version or bundle below.

  • https://www.sap.com/products/try-sap/trials-downloads.html 
  • https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/ 
  • https://www.sap.com/dashboard/demos.html

Explore powerful demo 576 videos

Explore our library of product demo videos for all lines of business, company sizes, industries and more.

  • https://www.sap.com/products/try-sap.html?sort=latest_desc 
Demo TitleDemo Description
AI-ready Sustainability: A Unified Layer for Finance Decisions
Don’t rebuild reporting as regulations change or guess at decarbonization levers. AI agents automate reporting - creating data points and linking metrics to disclosures with traceable lineage. Find out how you can use these agents to identify carbon hotspots, simulate scenarios, and recommend actions balancing cost and risk.This showcase was created for SAP Sapphire 2026 and includes existing product features as well as labs previews.
AI-enabled Product Operations: Material Choices as a Competitive AdvantageBy unifying product design data, ERP transactions, carbon footprints, and regulatory requirements, AI agents help supply chain teams proactively select materials that reduce cost volatility, minimize compliance risk, and lower emissions. Discover how agents can help eliminate issues before they show up in sourcing, manufacturing, or distribution.This showcase was created for SAP Sapphire 2026 and includes existing product features as well as labs previews.
From Insight to Safety: Using AI, Robotics, and real-time EHSSee workplace safety come to life with an embodied AI experience enabled by the SAP Environment, Health, and Safety Management application (EHS). Learn how intelligent robots and the Workplace Safety Agent capture frontline safety observations, detect risks, and trigger real-time EHS workflows - turning insights into faster, safer, and more confident decisions.This showcase was created for SAP Sapphire 2026 and includes existing product features as well as labs previews.
Improve the Candidate ExperienceUnderstand how to improve the candidate experience and create better recruitment outcomes by empowering talent acquisition leaders with real-time insights to close talent gaps in the hiring process. Together, SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting and SAP Candidate Experience by Qualtrics provide recruiters with a holistic view of the talent attraction and acquisition process with feedback opportunities embedded at every moment that matters in the candidate journey. Drive increased candidate conversion, optimize your talent funnel, and improve your employer brand reputation with SAP Candidate Experience.
Workload and Capacity Planning in SAP Field Service and Asset ManagementWatch how planners review workload, compare team capacity, and prioritize maintenance and service work in SAP Field Service and Asset Management to support faster, more balanced scheduling decisions.
International Trade Classification AgentThis agent helps product classification specialists classify goods for shipping. The agent reasons over product characteristics, trade regulations and acts to classify goods for international shipping. The agent recommends customs tariff numbers and commodity codes, providing rationale for easy review and approval. This agent will help ensure compliance with global trade regulations and reduce the risk of manual errors with robust governance and a clear, audit-ready decision
Financial Planning Assistant videoFocus on Enterprise Planning and Simulation Agent
SAP embodied AI x Environment Health and Safety Management (EHS) visual inspectionSAP Embodied AI enhances workplace safety by enabling robots to continuously monitor environments, detect hazards in real time, and adapt to changing conditions beyond scheduled inspections. Integrated with SAP Joule and EHS systems, the robots convert safety policies into tasks, identify risks such as trip hazards or blocked exits, and automatically log observations with supporting data and insights. This creates a closed-loop process from detection to resolution, improving compliance, accelerating response times, and reducing manual effort while promoting a proactive safety culture.
Asset and Service Assistant - Lab Preview

Watch the Lab Preview Demo from the Asset and Service Assistant.This assistant enables autonomous asset management by orchestrating end-to-end execution across roles – from detection to work to financial outcome. It converts anomalies, alerts, and requests into prioritized work packages, reserves parts, optimizes schedules, and guides technicians on mobile with AI briefings and diagnostics.

Demo: Utilities Customer Self-Service Agent

Watch in this hands-on demo how the Self-Service Agent for Utilities work and how it can support your customer service!

SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance

This is an overview of the SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance solution. It will review the how to drive compliance and how to flexibly manage your supply base.

SAP Sapphire 2026: Autonomous Asset Management

During major events, stadium infrastructures operate under intense pressure. Tens of thousands of visitors depend on critical systems, including power, connectivity, water, security, and facility operations to perform flawlessly without interruption. To meet these demands while balancing cost, uptime, and operational risk, organizations are increasingly adopting autonomous asset management powered by AI-driven orchestration across assets, workforce, and supply chain operations.Autonomous Asset Management eliminates siloed systems and inconsistent manual decision-making by combining AI, knowledge graphs, predictive intelligence, and end-to-end automation. This enables organizations to orchestrate assets, maintenance operations, health and safety processes, and connected supply chains through a unified operational framework.

Show less
Deep research capabilities in Joule + SAP Sales Cloud + SAP Service Cloud use caseA Sales Account Executive uses deep research capabilties in Joule to build an Account Plan. Deep research connects to SAP Sales Cloud, SAP Service Cloud, and live web search to automatically gather, analyze, and synthesize enterprise and external data into a transparent, structured plan. Using natural language prompts, sales teams can rapidly generate strategic insights, opportunity analysis, and action plans grounded in real SAP data—without switching systems or filling templates.
AI-Assisted Generation of Formatting RulesIn the SAP Integrated Business Planning, add-in for Microsoft Excel (SAP IBP, add-in for Microsoft Excel), you can generate SAP IBP formatting rules with the help of SAP Integrated Business Planning, add-in for Microsoft Excel, AI-assisted planning (planning assistance).
SAP Field Service and Asset Management - Demo VideoThis demo introduces SAP Field Service and Asset Management as an AI-powered execution layer for asset and service operations. It shows how teams can move from reactive work to connected, intelligent execution by unifying asset and service information, ERP context, workload capacity, scheduling, dispatching, and mobile execution.
End-to-End Agent Lifecycle DemoThis video goes over through the end-to-end lifecycle of an AI agent
SAP Document Presentment by OpenText VideoDiscover how organizations can design and deliver personalized communications across multiple channels, enabling your teams to create more relevant customer experiences while maintaining consistency and control.
SAP Cloud ERP for Professional ServicesSAP Cloud ERP connects the full professional services lifecycle in one integrated system, from opportunity and resource planning to project delivery, billing, and financial close. With embedded analytics and real-time visibility across every stage, it helps organizations improve utilization, strengthen financial control, and make smarter decisions throughout the project journey.
Adding Participants in SAP Ariba Guided SourcingExplore how adding participants in SAP Ariba Guided Sourcing enables seamless supplier engagement by inviting the right external contacts to sourcing events—ensuring competitive responses, efficient collaboration, and well-informed decision-making. By leveraging structured supplier search, contact creation, and lifecycle integration, organizations can optimize participation, balance competition, and drive better sourcing outcomes in real time.
SAP Integration Suite Vision OverviewExplore the SAP Integration Suite vision for agentic AI and learn how a trusted integration foundation can help enterprises connect AI agents across systems, data, and processes, govern how they operate, and accelerate agent integration with embedded AI assistance
SAP Cloud ERP: Setup and StaffSAP Cloud ERP supports the setup and staff phase of professional services by helping teams structure projects, align billing, and assign the right resources with confidence. With visibility into project details, staffing needs, skills matching, and resource utilization, it enables organizations to prepare projects effectively and move into execution with greater control and readiness.
SAP S/4HANA Finance: Intercompany Matching and ReconciliationSAP S/4HANA Cloud streamlines intercompany matching and reconciliation with real-time visibility, automated matching, and built-in workflows. By helping teams quickly identify discrepancies, manage exceptions, and track approvals within one system, it improves accuracy, reduces manual effort, and supports a faster, more efficient close process.
SAP Cloud ERP: Deliver and RecognizeSAP Cloud ERP helps organizations manage project delivery and revenue recognition in one connected system. By supporting time and expense capture, work-in-progress visibility, what-if simulations, and event-based revenue recognition, it enables teams to track project performance in real time, improve financial accuracy, and keep delivery and financial outcomes aligned.
SAP Cloud ERP: Sell and PlanSAP Cloud ERP supports the sell and plan phase of professional services by helping teams define cost and billing rates, evaluate margins early, and quickly create structured projects from proven templates. By connecting pricing, project planning, and resource visibility in one system, it enables more confident decisions and a smoother transition from opportunity to delivery
SAP S/4HANA Finance: Accounts PayableSAP S/4HANA Cloud streamlines accounts payable with centralized invoice management, automated three-way matching, and secure payment processing. With real-time dashboards, key KPIs, and intelligent automation, it helps teams improve accuracy, reduce manual effort, strengthen control, and gain better visibility into cash flow and AP performance.
SAP Commerce Cloud: Purpose-Built to Simplify B2B CommerceB2B buying experiences should be simple, even if the products aren’t. Connect processes, drive revenue, retain customers, and grow with a B2B customer experience (CX) that’s simple, even for your most complex products.
Maximize Marketing ROI with SAP Analytics CloudSAP Analytics Cloud Planning delivers a dedicated marketing budget planning feature, incorporating top-tier practices from leading CPG companies. This innovation empowers marketing leaders to craft and oversee marketing agendas, campaigns, and initiatives, both top-down and bottom-up, enriched with intuitive analytical visualizations.

SAP Demand Driven (MRP) - Optimize Inventory Level and Secure Customer serviceSAP Demand-Driven MRP (DDMRP) is an advanced supply chain planning approach designed to optimize inventory levels while ensuring high customer service levels. It helps businesses manage material flow more efficiently by responding to real demand signals rather than relying solely on traditional forecast-based planning.Key Features & Benefits:Demand-Driven Buffer Management:Uses strategic decoupling points in the supply chain to buffer inventory where needed.Helps reduce lead times and prevent stockouts or excess inventory.Optimized Inventory Levels:Avoids the bullwhip effect by dynamically adjusting inventory positions.Reduces working capital while ensuring product availability.Improved Customer Service:Aligns production and supply with actual market demand.Ensures on-time delivery by maintaining optimal stock levels.Adaptive Planning & Execution:Incorporates real-time demand signals to drive production and replenishment.Uses color-coded buffer zones (green, yellow, red) for inventory control.
SAP’s Two-Tier ERP: Streamlining Warranty Claims Across Headquarters and SubsidiariesSAP's Two-Tier ERP strategy with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition strengthens collaboration between headquarters and subsidiaries by automating warranty claims, validating customer and vendor warranties, and streamlining reimbursements for a faster, more transparent process.

SAP S/4HANA - Working CapitalAP S/4HANA optimizes working capital by improving cash flow, inventory management, and accounts payable/receivable processes. It provides real-time insights and automation to streamline financial operations and enhance liquidity. This enables businesses to maintain stability and invest in growth opportunities effectively.
Best Run Multi-CurrencySAP S/4HANA streamlines the management of exchange rates and multi-currency transactions, crucial in a global economy. It enables accurate reporting, seamless revaluation, and effective transaction management across currencies. This enhances financial transparency and minimizes risks from currency fluctuations. ​
SAP S/4HANA for Enterprise Contract ManagementSAP S/4HANA for Enterprise Contract Management provides a centralized contract repository integrated with ERP systems for real-time visibility. It enables easy search, tracking of contractual obligations, and proactive management of expiring agreements. This streamlines operations, improves compliance, and enhances contract oversight.
External TaxesSAP S/4HANA integrates with external tax engines to manage indirect tax compliance in multi-jurisdictional environments. This solution simplifies adapting to evolving tax laws as companies expand into new markets. It ensures accuracy and control, helping tax professionals stay compliant effortlessly.
Cash ManagementSAP S/4HANA Cash Management provides real-time financial data for accurate cash flow monitoring and decision-making. It allows users to identify and resolve issues, such as missing bank information, promptly. This enhances visibility and control over daily cash positions, improving financial efficiency.
The Continuous Financial CloseSAP S/4HANA streamlines the financial closing process by automating tasks, improving precision, and reducing compliance risks. It provides a comprehensive overview of closing tasks and metrics, enabling quick issue identification and resolution. This enhances efficiency, auditability, and financial accuracy during the close
SAP Profitability and Performance Management (PaPM)SAP’s Profitability and Performance Management (PaPM) helps businesses address regulatory pressures on transfer pricing and compliance challenges. It supports operational transfer pricing with robust planning and defense mechanisms. This ensures compliance, reduces risks, and enhances financial transparency under OECD guidelines. ​​
SAP S/4HANA Goods Receipt and Invoice Receipt (GR/IR)SAP S/4HANA streamlines Goods Receipt and Invoice Receipt (GR/IR) reconciliation using machine learning and efficient processes. It eliminates period-close bottlenecks by providing real-time data and easy access to relevant business information. This improves efficiency and effectiveness throughout the financial period.
SAP S/4HANA's Financial Engine - Document SplittingSAP S/4HANA's Financial Engine introduces document splitting to balance financial statements at granular levels, such as segment or profit center. This ensures that debits and credits across GL accounts remain equal while meeting reporting requirements. The feature simplifies financial reporting and improves accuracy for organizations with complex structures
Verifying General EntriesThe SAP workflow for verifying general journal entries ensures accuracy before posting by involving specific roles. Requesters create and submit entries, while processors verify and approve them within the system. This process enhances control and accountability in financial data management. ​

SAP Digital AccessDigital Access Evaluation Service is one of various services provided by SAP's Global Adoption Insights & License Compliance (GLAC). With this free-of-charge service, SAP offers their customers support to determine the estimated initial number of digital access documents.Direct human access to the Digital Core systems (ERP & SAP S/4HANA) will continue to be licensed on the number of users, while according to SAP's Digital Access pricing model, all indirect access will be charged based on the number of documents associated to nine specific Document types.Based on a detailed evaluation of the relevant document types, customers will have a basis for the decision whether SAP Digital Access is the suitable license model for the future. The result can be used as a baseline for the Digital Access Adoption Program.

SAP Business Integrity Screening: Detect Suspicious TransactionsWhen the BIS application was first built, it was actually two distinct SAP applications to support two distinct use cases, namely Fraud Management Vs. Business Partner Screening. Today there is one Material Code. The issue is, the use cases (Fraud Management Vs. Business Partner Screening) are very different. Put another way, a customer that has requirements that the “fraud detection” piece of BIS handles, has no want need nor desire for the partner screening piece and vice versa. This first video will profile the fraud detection piece of BIS.

Contract Central RepositoryProvide one central repository; Increase adoption leveraging SAP Ariba Contracts
Ensuring Contract ComplianceEnsure contract compliance; Increase savings leveraging SAP Ariba.
RFx / Event ManagementStreamline processing; Drive user adoption leveraging SAP Ariba.
Drive Efficiency with Contract TemplatesDrive user adoption; Reduce cycle times leveraging SAP Ariba Contracts
Supplier NegotiationIncrease processing efficiency; Increase speed to drive value leveraging SAP Ariba.
Approvals ManagementEliminate manual approvals and Remove bottlenecks leveraging SAP Ariba.
Incident Analysis for AutomotiveThis demo video shows an art of the possible style demo video that helps take our solutions to the next level to address automotive needs.
SAP Analytics Cloud: Incident Analysis for Industrial ManufacturingIncident Analysis in the Industrial Manufacturing industry with SAP Analytics Cloud
SAP Analytics Cloud: Incident Analysis for High TechIncident Analysis for the High Tech industry using SAP Analytics Cloud
SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting: How It WorksSAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting accurately reports and adheres to standards for external organizations and stakeholders to predict and forecast revenue. Tackle the complexity associated with SAP.
SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Navigation overviewWith SAP S/4HANA Cloud, get a bird's-eye view of your organization, or dive deep into the details to intelligently guide your financial decision making.
SAP S/4HANA: Advanced Returns with Direct Ship to VendorWith SAP leverage S/4HANA Advance Returns with Direct Ship to reduce costs related to customer returns processing, increase organizational efficiency, and improve customer satisfaction.
SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Customer ManagementSAP S/4HANA Cloud makes Creating Customer Master simple and accurate by streamlining data entry.
The Value of RISE with SAP for the CFOThis video summarizes the Value of RISE with SAP for the CFO
SAP Profitability and Performance Management: Green IT Cost ManagementTake positive steps towards more sustainable IT practices. Move towards a lighter carbon footprint and potentially lower energy costs by improving server utilization. Utilize IT equipment adapted to the precise requirements, optimize preset configurations in power management, or move to cloud computing.
SAP Business Network and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Collaboration OverviewThe SAP Business Network makes it easy for buyers and suppliers to collaborate on transactions and strengthen relationships by connecting to SAP S/4HANA Cloud through a single standardized channel
SAP Responsible Design and ProductionCalculate extended producer responsibility (EPR) obligations, plastic taxes, and corporate commitments to optimize material choices. To learn more, visit www.sap.com/rdp
SAP S/4HANA: Central Purchase Contract ProcessingSee how SAP S/4HANA Sourcing and Procurement can help to reduce procurement costs by harnessing your buying power using central purchase contracts.
Simplified Direct Operating Cost ManagementFor the first time in the airline industry, there's a comprehensive business and technological solution that enables airlines to manage Direct Operational Charges and the respective business processes within a consolidated SAP system.
SAP GRC Solutions: Integrated Risk and Controls Management for SAP S4HANAIn this video, you will see how SAP GRC Solutions helps you improve compliance, optimize audit resource utilization, and mitigate risk more efficiently.
SAP Intelligent Asset Management: Enterprise product development visualizationSAP Intelligent Asset Management provides you a visual index for your equipment data, with the ability to see all your equipment from the comfort of your device!
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Advanced Real-Time Production PlanningSAP S/4HANA Cloud uses machine learning to enable advanced real-time production planning to predict demand and ensure optimized supply.
SAP Insurance: Incentive and Commission Management for InsuranceThis is an overview demonstration of Incentive and Commission Management for Insurance.
SAP Commodity Management: Agribusiness overviewIn this overview, you will learn how SAP Agricultural Contract Management and SAP Commodity Risk Management enable you to run your agricultural commodity business on an end-to-end platform that helps you cut costs and mitigate risk.
SAP Global Trade Services: Sanctioned party list screeningsLearn how a sales order can be blocked for a denied party hit, and then cleared in GTS.
SAP Treasury and Risk Management: Best run multi-currencyManage the impact of currency fluctuations on transactions and accurately report on a full range of currencies.
Three lines of defense: SAP Process ControlThis demo video showcases how you can leverage SAP solutions to manage all controls and compliance processes.
SAP S/4HANA Finance: Intercompany matching and reconciliationThis video showcases how your Finance organization can leverage SAP S/4HANA for transactional-level matching in real-time - eliminating cumbersome ETL processes.
SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Margin analysisWatch this demo to see how with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you learn from past successes, pooling your transactions for a clear understanding of costs and profits – on a single platform.

SAP Developers can instantly dive into SAP’s ecosystem with free trials, watch powerful demos and downloads of powerful tools like SAP BTP, SAP HANA, and SAPUI5—giving you hands-on access to enterprise-grade platforms, AI SDKs, and responsive app frameworks without upfront cost.

What Makes It Interesting

  • No upfront cost: Free trials lower the barrier to entry.
  • Enterprise relevance: Skills learned here are directly applicable to real-world corporate environments.
  • Cutting-edge tech: Access to AI, cloud, and advanced UI frameworks.
  • Community & support: SAP provides documentation, forums, and updates to help developers succeed.

Explore our most popular downloads, or search for a specific version.

entry.title: Product-Agent Fit: A Practical Framework for Building on SAP BTP in the Age of Joule
entry.content:
A practical framework for SAP architects, developers, and product managers navigating the agentic enterprise

The Question SAP Is Already Asking

At SAP TechEd 2025 in Berlin, SAP Executive Board Member Muhammad Alam, in charge of SAP Product & Engineering, said SAP was entering “the era of agentic AI, where AI moves from being just a tool to becoming your trusted teammate."

A few months later, on March 31, 2026, SAP CEO Christian Klein went further in his blog: "Software is becoming the operating system for trusted autonomy." (Klein, March: “Software is Entering Its Most Powerful Era”)

These are not marketing statements. They signal architectural direction — and they have direct implications for every SAP developer, consultant, and product manager building on BTP today.

Here is the question they force us to ask about every application we build or extend:

Not only "will a user love this?" — but also "will Joule reach for it?"

That stretch — from human satisfaction to agent invocability — is what I want to explore in this post through a framework I have been developing: Product-Agent Fit (PAF).

 

What Is Product-Agent Fit?

For nearly two decades, the dominant lens for evaluating software products has been Product-Market Fit (PMF) — the degree to which a product satisfies a human market's needs well enough that users adopt it, pay for it, and recommend it. PMF assumes the validator of product value is always a human being with preferences, emotions, and tolerance for rough edges.

Product-Agent Fit (PAF) proposes an additional validator: the AI agent.

PAF is the degree to which a product's capability surface is naturally consumable by AI agents — such that agents discover it autonomously, invoke it correctly, trust its outputs, chain it into workflows, and operate within enterprise governance constraints — without human mediation at invocation time.

In SAP terms: does your CAP application, your Fiori app, your S/4HANA extension — does it work when Joule calls it? Not when a user clicks through it. When an agent invokes it.

The answer to that question is increasingly one of the most important singlas you would know about the long-term relevance of whatever you are building.

 

Why This Matters Specifically on SAP BTP

SAP has been executing a clear, multi-year trajectory toward agent-first architecture:

  • September 2023: Joule launched as a copilot — human-facing, UI-adjacent
  • October 2024: Joule went agentic — multi-agent orchestration, coordinating "across business functions" (TechEd 2024)
  • April 2025: SAP joined Google as a founding industry contributor to the A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol — standardizing how agents communicate across enterprise platforms
  • November 2025: SAP unveiled major advancements in agentic AI, including introducing new Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers at TechEd 2025, enabling Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, and Windsurf to access SAP development frameworks directly
  • March 2026: Christian Klein: "Building an agent is becoming increasingly easier—the tip of the iceberg. Deploying it across end-to-end supply chains or financial close processes, with full compliance and audit trails, is where most of the effort lies."

The direction is unambiguous. SAP is rebuilding its platform so that Joule — and agents beyond Joule — can consume every capability without depending on screen navigation.

The question for us as builders is: are the things we build on BTP keeping pace with that direction?

 

The PAF Framework — Mapped to SAP

I propose twelve dimensions for evaluating whether an SAP BTP application has achieved Product-Agent Fit. Each maps to specific SAP tools and patterns you can act on today.

Two SAP resources serve as the primary references throughout this framework. For agent-layer implementation — Joule Studio, MCP tooling, agent architecture, and the AI development lifecycle — SAP's AI Golden Path (architecture.learning.sap.com/docs/aigp) is the dedicated guide. For platform security, CAP patterns, ORD, and audit logging — the SAP BTP Developer's Guide applies. Where a dimension touches both layers, both are called out.

1. Capability Fit

Are your capabilities atomic enough for an agent to invoke directly?

In SAP terms: your OData V4 services, your CAP action handlers, your ABAP logic exposed as OData V4 services via RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model) — are they scoped at the level of a single, invocable agent action? Not "manage the purchase order" but release_purchase_order(po_id, approver_id).

The 2,100+ pre-delivered Joule skills SAP has published by the end of 2025 are each scoped exactly this way. If you are building custom extensions, your capability granularity should match that standard.

SAP tool: CAP action definitions, OData V4 services built on RAP

2. Interface Fit

Is your API surface natively machine-readable without human explanation?

This is where MCP comes in. At TechEd 2025, SAP announced that developers using agentic coding tools can now access SAP development frameworks via SAP Build local MCP Servers. The open-source SAP MDK MCP Server (github.com/SAP/mdk-mcp-server) exposes Mobile Development Kit tools as MCP tools consumable by authorized MCP-compatible agents.

For your custom BTP application, Interface Fit means: is there a machine-readable, typed schema that describes what your service does, what parameters it takes, and what errors it returns — in a form an LLM can reason over without a human explaining it?

SAP tools: MCP Server definitions, OData V4 with typed EDM schemas, SAP AI SDK (Javascript: github.com/SAP/ai-sdk-js; Java: github.com/SAP/ai-sdk-java)

3. Reliability Fit

Are your outputs deterministic enough for agents to chain without verification overhead?

Christian Klein stated this precisely in his March 2026 blog post: "orchestration, policy enforcement, and workflow determinism are the gatekeepers of trust."

In practice, this means your business logic — especially anything involving financial calculations, inventory, or compliance — must be deterministic and auditable. Joule should call your service and trust the result. It should not need to re-verify the output or ask a human to confirm before passing it to the next step.

This is the separation-of-concerns principle that CAP embodies, now applied to agent consumption: your service layer handles determinism; Joule handles the reasoning. Do not mix these responsibilities.

SAP tool: CAP handlers with deterministic business logic, CDS-defined validation rules

4. Context Fit

Can Joule determine when and how to invoke your service from its schema alone?

SAP's Knowledge Graph (announced TechEd 2024) maps "relationships and context across SAP's vast data landscape" using fifty years of business process expertise — so Joule knows the semantics of your business domain, not just the syntax of your API.

For your custom application, this means: tool descriptions must be semantically rich enough for an LLM to correctly select your tool from a catalog of hundreds. A tool named processMaterial with no description will not be selected. A tool named check_material_availability with the description "Returns current stock level and ATP date for a given material in a specified plant" will be.

SAP tools: MCP tool descriptions, Joule Studio skill definitions, CDS annotations

5. Composability Fit

Does your service output feed directly into the next Joule step without transformation?

SAP's industry partnership in the A2A protocol (April 2025) is a direct investment in this dimension. A2A is intended to enable Joule to coordinate with agents from Google, Microsoft, and other vendors — "sharing context and acting together across enterprise platforms without requiring manual system switching or data loss."

For your application: every service response should be structured JSON that the next tool in the chain can consume directly. If your response is only a formatted HTML string or a PDF attachment, Joule cannot chain it — a human must intervene. That is a composability failure.

SAP tools: Structured CDS projections, A2A-compliant agent registration, Joule Studio orchestration

6. Headless Deployment Fit

Does your application deliver full value without a human opening a browser?

SAP repositioned Joule from AI copilot to "AI orchestrator across your entire organization" (February 2025) — meaning Joule should be able to execute complete workflows end-to-end, not just assist a human who is already on a screen.

Ask this about your application: if I remove the Fiori UI entirely, can Joule still accomplish the core business task? If the answer is no — because some step requires a human to click Confirm in the UI — that is a Headless Deployment Fit gap.

The fix is not to remove the UI. It is to ensure that human confirmation steps are exposed as structured approval actions that agents can surface through conversation, not as UI buttons that block the workflow.

SAP tools: CAP action handlers callable by MCP, Joule-invocable approval flows on SAP Build Work Zone

7. Trust Pipeline Fit

Does your application enforce governance at the API layer, not just the UI layer?

Walter Sun, SVP and Global Head of AI at SAP, has been direct: "accountability must always rest with humans or legal entities, never with the AI itself" (Sun, June 2025: “From Assistive AI to Agentic AI: Risks, Responsibilities, and the Road Ahead”). And Christian Klein identifies "validation rules, compliance checks, approval flows, identity management and audit trails" as non-negotiable requirements for enterprise agents (Klein, March 2026).

In practice, this means authorization enforcement must happen at your CAP service layer — not just at the Fiori UI. For new applications, this applies whether you are using XSUAA or the newer SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) and Authorization Management Service (AMS) stack that the SAP BTP Developer's Guide now recommends; the governance requirement is identical under both. An agent that bypasses the UI must encounter the same authorization gates as a human user. Every agent-invoked write should be logged to the SAP Audit Log Service with full provenance.

If your authorization logic lives only in the Fiori app and your CAP services are essentially open to any authenticated caller, you have a Trust Pipeline Fit gap — and a serious security risk in an agentic world.

SAP tools: Authorization enforcement in CAP handlers, SAP Audit Log Service, SAP Authorization and Trust Management

8. Longitudinal Context Fit

Does your application give Joule access to accumulated business knowledge, not just current-state data?

SAP's Business Data Cloud creates "a single trusted data layer" across SAP and non-SAP sources. The SAP Knowledge Graph leverages "50-plus years of business process expertise" to give agents semantic understanding of business relationships that raw data cannot provide.

For your custom application: does Joule know that this supplier has had three late deliveries in the past six months? Does it know that this customer's contract expires in 30 days? Does it understand the regulatory history of this tax position? If that context is locked in a data warehouse that Joule cannot query, your application cannot achieve longitudinal context fit.

SAP tools: SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine (for RAG), SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine, SAP Analytics Cloud, CDS-defined analytical views

9. Surface-Agnostic Rendering Fit

Does your application reach the human wherever they are, not only inside Fiori?

Joule has been extended beyond SAP applications into third-party applications via WalkMe integration (SAP Sapphire, 2025), and Slack adoption for SAP agent interactions has grown significantly. The underlying principle is the same one Salesforce articulates for headless commerce and CRM — that the engagement surface can change (mobile app, Slack, Teams, voice interface) while the underlying business platform stays consistent: "The surface changes. The platform doesn't."

For your application: can the agent surface your workflow outcomes as a Slack notification, a Teams card, a mobile push alert — without you having to build a separate integration for each? If your application only surfaces results in Fiori, you are missing the engagement surfaces where your users actually spend their time.

SAP tools: SAP Build Work Zone, Joule in Slack integration, SAP Mobile Services, SAP BTP notification services

10. Agent Operational Maturity Fit

Can you detect and fix Joule behavioral drift without waiting for a user complaint?

SAP ships the LeanIX AI Agent Hub — dashboards showing agent deployment, process impact, and performance metrics. This is the SAP-side equivalent of what Salesforce calls its Testing Center and Observability tooling.

The critical insight from the industry: agents are probabilistic, not deterministic. There is no bug to fix — only behavior to observe and tune. If your Joule skill works correctly today but drifts after the next model update or business rule change, you need operational tooling to detect that drift before it affects production processes.

SAP tools: LeanIX AI Agent Hub, SAP AI Core evaluation service, Joule Studio agent testing

11. Marketplace Discovery Fit

Is your application discoverable by agents, not just by humans browsing the SAP Marketplace?

ORD — Open Resource Discovery is SAP's answer to this dimension. Now governed by the Linux Foundation Europe and NeoNephos Foundation (open-resource-discovery.org), ORD enables applications to "self-describe their exposed resources and capabilities" in a standardized, decentralized format. When your BTP application publishes ORD metadata, Joule can discover and compose your capabilities without a developer manually wiring the integration.

If your application is not publishing ORD metadata, it is invisible to the next generation of agent-based orchestration — regardless of how well it works when a human finds it manually.

SAP tools: ORD specification implementation in CAP, SAP Business Accelerator Hub publication, Joule Studio as a unified catalog and registration system 

12. Execution Transparency Fit

A dimension that emerged from deeper analysis after this post was first published.

Do your service outputs tell an agent what kind of result they are?

There is a distinction human users apply almost unconsciously, but agents cannot reliably infer without explicit declaration: the difference between a deterministic result — for example, a confirmed database write, a rule evaluation, or a calculated inventory figure — and a probabilistic result — for example, an LLM-generated summary, recommendation, or classification.

A human reading your application output usually infers this from context. An agent should not have to. If Joule calls your CAP service and receives a response, it needs a machine-readable signal indicating whether that result can be chained directly into the next workflow step, or whether it should pass through verification first.

Without that signal, the agent is forced into one of two bad choices: treat every output as trusted, allowing errors to compound silently downstream; or treat every output as uncertain, adding unnecessary verification overhead to deterministic service results that do not need it.

This is Execution Transparency Fit: the degree to which your product provides machine-readable metadata that distinguishes deterministic, rules-based, or transactionally confirmed outputs from probabilistic AI-generated outputs — enabling Joule to apply appropriate trust, verification, and chaining behavior without human mediation at the boundary.

Deterministic does not mean infallible. It means the result was produced by a known service contract, rule, calculation, or transaction boundary rather than by probabilistic model generation.

On SAP BTP, this boundary already exists structurally. CAP handlers typically produce typed EDM results derived from HANA Cloud queries, CDS-defined validation, and rule-based business logic. SAP AI SDK calls, by contrast, may produce LLM-generated outputs whose quality depends on prompts, model behavior, grounding, and evaluation logic. But unless the service contract exposes that distinction, the consuming agent sees only another response payload.

The fix is a metadata convention developers can adopt today. Add a provenance object to the MCP tool result’s structuredContent, and declare it in the tool’s outputSchema.

For deterministic operations — for example, standard CAP CRUD handlers or rule-based CAP actions:

{
  "result": { "...": "..." },
  "provenance": {
    "execution_type": "deterministic",
    "confidence": null,
    "verification_recommended": false
  }
}

For probabilistic operations — for example, a CAP action that calls the SAP AI SDK:

{
  "result": { "...": "..." },
  "provenance": {
    "execution_type": "probabilistic",
    "confidence": 0.84,
    "model": "<model-or-deployment-name>",
    "verification_recommended": true
  }
}

A comparable industry signal is Salesforce’s Agent Script, introduced with Agentforce 360 as a human-readable representation for defining agent behavior and making agent execution more predictable. The broader lesson is not that SAP developers should copy Salesforce syntax, but that enterprise platforms are moving toward explicit execution contracts for agents.

In SAP terms, this metadata could be declared today in MCP tool definitions, structured outputs, and output schemas — and potentially later through a CDS annotation such as @executionType if SAP or the community standardizes such a convention. That gap is worth raising against the SAP AI Golden Path and related agent development guidance.

A developer who adopts this pattern today is ahead of the platform standard — but every enterprise IT, audit, and security reviewer will eventually ask for it.

SAP tools: CAP action handlers, MCP tool definitions, SAP AI SDK, SAP Audit Log Service

 

The SAP PAF Maturity Ladder

Where does your application sit today?

Level 0 — INVISIBLE

  UI-only. No OData/CAP API. Joule cannot reach it.

  Example: Legacy Excel-based processes, classic Dynpro without API layer.

 

Level 1 — REACHABLE

  OData API exists but no ORD metadata, no MCP tools defined.

  Joule can be manually wired but won't discover it autonomously.

  Example: Standard CAP app without ORD publication.

 

Level 2 — CALLABLE

  OData V4 with typed schemas.

 Joule can invoke with explicit developer instruction. Errors are structured.

  Example: CAP app following the SAP BTP Developer's Guide, ORD partially configured.

 

Level 3 — DISCOVERABLE

  ORD metadata published. MCP tools defined in line with the SAP AI Golden Path.

  Joule Studio or other agent tooling can discover and wire the capability with much less manual effort.

  Example: Full SAP BTP Developer's Guide + SAP AI Golden Path + MCP + ORD.

 

Level 4 — PREFERRED

  Joule selects autonomously. Authorization enforced at CAP layer.

  Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) approval flows agent-accessible.

  Longitudinal context available via Business Data Cloud.

  Registered as A2A-compatible via SAP Agent Fabric / BTP agent runtime.

  Example: Production Joule Agent with full BTP governance.

 

Level 5 — FOUNDATIONAL

  Other Joule agents and third-party agents depend on yours as a building block.

  Discoverable within Joule Studio without manual wiring.

  Example: What SAP's own HANA Cloud and Master Data services are candidates to become.

 

What to Do This Sprint

With your application's level on the PAF Maturity Ladder identified, here is a concrete sprint-level action plan for each transition.

Moving from Level 1 to Level 2: Publish your CAP service with a typed OData V4 schema. Define structured error responses using CAP's error handling framework. Ensure every action has a meaningful, LLM-readable description in your CDS annotations.

Moving from Level 2 to Level 3: Implement ORD metadata in your CAP application. Define at least one MCP tool wrapping your most-used CAP action. SAP's AI Golden Path (architecture.learning.sap.com/docs/aigp) is the recommended starting point for MCP tool definitions, Joule Studio registration, and the agent development lifecycle. Test that Joule can invoke your tool without a human explaining the parameters, with structured errors and audit trace (in natural language).

Moving from Level 3 to Level 4: Move authorization enforcement into your CAP service handlers — not just Fiori app routing. Add Audit Log Service integration for every agent-invoked write. Design your human approval flows as CAP actions that return structured confirmation tokens, not (just) as UI buttons. Register your agent as A2A-compatible via SAP Agent Fabric or the BTP agent runtime to enable cross-vendor agent interoperability.

Moving from Level 4 to Level 5: Document your MCP tool definitions in a format LLMs can parse. Publish relevant APIs, packages, or reusable assets where appropriate. Consider submitting your Joule skill to the SAP partner ecosystem so other agent stacks can discover and reuse your capability.

 

The Framing That Changes How You Build

I want to close with Christian Klein's formulation, because I think it is the most important framing for every SAP practitioner today:

"Building an agent is becoming increasingly easier—the tip of the iceberg. Deploying it across end-to-end supply chains or financial close processes, with full compliance and audit trails, is where most of the effort lies."

That sentence captures the challenge precisely. The hard part is not building the agent. The hard part is making your platform ready for the agent — so that when Joule arrives at your CAP service, your OData endpoint, your approval workflow, it finds something it can invoke, trust, govern, and chain.

That readiness is Product-Agent Fit.

And based on the trajectory of SAP's own platform — A2A, MCP, ORD, Joule Studio, LeanIX AI Agent Hub — it is clear that SAP is building many PAF-like requirements into the platform layer. The question is whether the applications we build on top of that platform are keeping pace.

Over to You

I am curious where the SAP community currently stands on this:

  • Are you already designing your BTP applications with Joule invocability in mind, or is agent consumption still a future concern?
  • Has your team published ORD metadata or defined MCP tools yet?
  • Where do most of your current applications sit on the PAF Maturity Ladder?

Drop your thoughts in the comments. The more specific the better — I would love to hear concrete patterns, blockers, and solutions from those already building at the frontier.

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2026: Registration is live!
entry.content:
SAP TechEd’s 30th anniversary!

Can you believe that SAP TechEd turns thirty this year?  Let’s party like it’s 1996 (portable CD Discman anyone?!)  In honor of all the great TechEd moments, we’ll take a trip down memory lane with you, celebrating TechEd’s legacy as the premier technical event for developers in the past, the present, and especially into the future.

We’ll be back in Berlin this October with the full-fledged, in-person, old school, TechEd experience you know and love. So, what’s in the works?  You name it: Hands-on Workshops, Strategy Talks, Road Maps, Deep Dives, Demo Stations and my favorite, a full-blown Community Clubhouse with fan favorites like the Developer Garage and the Community Theater. Berlin is your place to connect with SAP experts, Developer Advocates and your TechEd friends and neighbors in the flesh! TechEd fans rejoice!

What if I’m looking for more of a “sweatpants and sofa” vibe?

Fear not, my friend!  Throughout the three days, we'll be livestreaming sessions from Berlin for our live virtual show.  Many of our in-person Berlin sessions will be streamed live and available on-demand, and we’ll also have a virtual Q&A experience, where experts field questions from both live and virtual audiences.  In fact, much of our virtual show will take place from our virtual studio right on the Berlin show floor, with exclusive segments designed for our virtual audience.  

What kinds of sessions can I expect?

Great question.  We'll have different session formats so you can choose how you prefer to learn. Want to sit back and hear all about SAP strategy from our experts?  Or are you looking to get your hands dirty with some coding?  Are you looking for a high-level overview or want to dive deep into a particular topic? The choice is totally yours.

As we all know, at the end of the day, SAP TechEd is about community above all else, and we’ve got so much planned to bring us together in Berlin:

Come hang with us!

It's all about connecting, learning, and celebrating our community. It’s SAP TechEd and we’re beyond pumped and honored to bring it all to you this October! Register today and let me know what you are looking forward to the most.
